title: Rasta Ray's Benna Beat
summary: Rasta Ray loves to drum! He learns from his Grandpa how to play the special Benna rhythm, and shares his music with everyone in Antigua.

pages.text: Rasta Ray lives in a bright yellow house in Antigua. He loves to play his drum, boom, boom, boom! He wants to learn to play real Caribbean music.
pages.imagePrompt: Illustration: Rasta Ray, a cheerful boy with dreadlocks, sits outside a bright yellow Antiguan house. Palm trees sway in the background. He's holding a small drum, smiling widely.

pages.text: Grandpa says, "Ray, come learn the Benna beat!" Benna is old-time Antiguan music. It makes everyone feel [irie] [feeling good].
pages.imagePrompt: Illustration: Rasta Ray sits at the feet of his Grandpa, who is an older man with a kind face and a big smile. Grandpa is holding a larger drum and demonstrating how to play. The scene is set on a porch overlooking the Caribbean Sea.

pages.text: Grandpa beats the drum, 'dum-ta-dum, dum-ta-dum'. Rasta Ray tries too! He’s getting better. Time for [nyam] [to eat] some mangoes!
pages.imagePrompt: Illustration: Close-up of Rasta Ray's hands on the drum, following Grandpa's instructions. A plate of juicy mangoes sits nearby. Colorful tropical flowers are visible.

pages.text: After he [nyams] [eats] mangoes, Ray plays the Benna beat for his friends. Boom! Boom! They dance and sing. Everyone feels the irie rhythm!
pages.imagePrompt: Illustration: Rasta Ray is playing his drum for a group of children, all smiling and dancing. They are surrounded by colorful houses and lush greenery. The setting should feel festive and joyful.

pages.text: Rasta Ray learned to share his music. Now everyone in Antigua can feel the irie Benna beat! What music makes YOU feel good?
pages.imagePrompt: Illustration: A wide shot of the village with happy people listening and dancing to Rasta Ray's music. The sun is setting, casting a warm glow over the scene. A sense of community and joy should be evident.
